expectations.text: form 상태/유효성 검증을 담당하는 훅 파일이 생성된다 (예: useLoginForm)
expectations.evidence: hooks/login/useLoginForm/index.ts was created containing form state (email, password, errors, generalError via useState) and validation logic (validate, handleEmailBlur, handlePasswordBlur using validateEmail/validatePassword).

expectations.text: submit/API 호출을 담당하는 훅 파일이 생성된다 (예: useLogin)
expectations.evidence: No separate submit/API hook file was created. The submit logic (handleSubmit calling login()) is embedded inside useLoginForm rather than extracted into a dedicated hook like useLogin. Only one hook file exists: hooks/login/useLoginForm/index.ts.

expectations.text: 훅 이름이 use 접두사로 시작한다 (tcf profile: hook prefix = use)
expectations.evidence: The hook is named useLoginForm, which starts with the 'use' prefix.
The hook file is at hooks/login/useLoginForm/index.ts, matching the {path}/{hookName}/index.ts pattern.

expectations.text: page.tsx에 직접적인 useState 호출이 남지 않는다
expectations.evidence: page.tsx contains no useState import or call. It only imports Link and useLoginForm.
page.tsx imports useLoginForm from '@/hooks/login/useLoginForm' (line 4) and destructures all values/handlers from it (lines 7-17).

expectations.text: 이벤트 핸들러가 handle 접두사를 사용한다 (shared profile: internal handler = handle*)
expectations.evidence: All event handlers use the handle prefix: handleEmailChange, handlePasswordChange, handleEmailBlur, handlePasswordBlur, handleSubmit.

expectations.text: 훅이 화살표 함수로 정의된다 (shared profile: function style = arrow)
expectations.evidence: The hook is defined as 'const useLoginForm = (): UseLoginFormReturn => { ... }' — an arrow function expression.
